last_digest.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
last_digest.rss_summary: France Titres, the government agency in France for issuing and managince administrative documents has disclosed a data breach after a threat actor claimed the attack and stealing citizen data. [...]

last_digest.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
last_digest.rss_summary: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

last_digest.title: Ransomware negotiator caught secretly assisting BlackCat extortion scheme
last_digest.rss_summary: Angelo Martino pleaded guilty to helping BlackCat ransomware group while acting as a ransomware negotiator. Another U.S. cybersecurity expert, Angelo Martino, admitted helping the BlackCat ransomware group while working as a ransomware negotiator. Angelo Martino (41) admitted helping the BlackCat ransomware group while working for a U.S. incident response firm. “A Florida man, formerly employed […]

last_digest.rss_summary:
The hackers targeted LayerZero’s DVN, compromising certain RPCs and DDoSing others to trigger failover to the poisoned infrastructure.

last_digest.title: Yet another ex-ransomware negotiator admits turning rogue after payoff from crimelords
last_digest.rss_summary: Plus: Court papers reveal nonprofit paid a ransom worth nearly $26.8 million The third of three former ransomware negotiators accused of assisting the ALPHV/BlackCat ransomware gang in extorting US businesses has pleaded guilty, months after his two co-workers did the same.…

last_week_digest.title: Rockstar Games receives “pay or leak” warning after cyberattack
last_week_digest.tldr: Rockstar Games confirmed a security breach after hackers accessed its Snowflake environment via a compromised third-party SaaS provider, Anodot. While the developer maintains that the incident involved limited, non-material data, the extortion attempt by the ShinyHunters group highlights the escalating risks of supply chain vulnerabilities. This event signals a broader industry trend where attackers bypass robust internal security by exploiting interconnected cloud services to gain unauthorized corporate access.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers claim breach of Rockstar Games via cloud analytics platform
last_week_digest.tldr: The cybercrime group ShinyHunters recently breached Rockstar Games by exploiting authentication tokens stolen from the cloud analytics platform Anodot. While Rockstar downplayed the incident as non-material, the attack highlights a broader supply-chain vulnerability affecting over a dozen organisations using Snowflake environments. This breach signals a critical shift in industry risk, where third-party service providers have become primary vectors for attackers targeting sensitive corporate data and intellectual property globally.

last_week_digest.title: OpenAI Impacted by North Korea-Linked Axios Supply Chain Hack
last_week_digest.tldr: OpenAI recently confirmed that a macOS code signing certificate was compromised following a supply chain attack linked to North Korean threat actors targeting the Axios platform. While the breach did not result in direct data loss for OpenAI users, the incident highlights critical vulnerabilities within software distribution pipelines. This event signals a growing industry trend where sophisticated state-sponsored groups increasingly exploit third-party dependencies to undermine global enterprise security.

last_week_digest.title: European Gym giant Basic-Fit data breach affects 1 million members
last_week_digest.tldr: European fitness giant Basic-Fit recently suffered a significant data breach, exposing the personal details of one million members across twelve countries. Attackers accessed sensitive information, including bank account numbers and contact data, highlighting critical vulnerabilities in centralised member databases. This incident underscores the growing risk to large-scale consumer service providers, signalling that even rapid internal detection cannot prevent substantial regulatory scrutiny and potential reputational damage within the European market.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers access Booking.com user data, company secures systems
last_week_digest.tldr: Booking.com recently confirmed a data breach where unauthorised parties accessed sensitive customer information, including names, email addresses, and reservation details. While payment data remained secure, the incident forced a mandatory reset of reservation PINs to mitigate further risk. This event highlights the persistent threat to global travel platforms, signalling an urgent need for enhanced third-party security oversight to prevent sophisticated phishing campaigns targeting customers via stolen booking data.

last_week_digest.title: Personal data of 1 million gym members compromised in Basic-Fit security incident
last_week_digest.tldr: Basic-Fit, Europe’s largest gym operator, recently suffered a significant data breach exposing the personal information of approximately one million members. Compromised details include names, addresses, bank account numbers, and contact information. This incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of large-scale consumer databases to unauthorised access. It serves as a stark reminder for the fitness industry that robust monitoring and rapid incident response are essential for protecting sensitive member data.

last_week_digest.title: Europe’s Largest Gym Chain Says Data Breach Impacts 1 Million Members
last_week_digest.tldr: Basic-Fit, Europe’s largest gym chain, recently suffered a significant data breach compromising the personal records of one million members. Attackers successfully exfiltrated sensitive information, including names, dates of birth, and banking details, creating severe operational risks and potential regulatory liabilities. This incident signals a growing trend of cybercriminals targeting consumer-facing service providers, highlighting the urgent necessity for robust data protection measures across the global fitness industry.

last_week_digest.title: McGraw-Hill confirms data breach following extortion threat
last_week_digest.tldr: McGraw-Hill confirmed a data breach resulting from a Salesforce misconfiguration, triggering extortion threats from the ShinyHunters group. While the company claims the exposed information is limited and non-sensitive, the attackers allege they possess 45 million records. This incident highlights critical systemic risks within third-party SaaS environments, demonstrating how configuration errors can expose global enterprises to significant reputational damage and extortion attempts despite robust internal security protocols.

last_week_digest.title: OpenAI expands its cyber defense program with GPT-5.4-Cyber for vetted researchers
last_week_digest.tldr: OpenAI is scaling its Trusted Access for Cyber program, granting thousands of verified defenders access to GPT-5.4-Cyber, a model fine-tuned for binary reverse engineering and vulnerability analysis. By lowering refusal boundaries for security professionals, the initiative aims to accelerate patch cycles and software resilience. This shift signals a broader industry trend toward providing defensive AI tools to combat sophisticated threats, balancing dual-use risks with critical infrastructure protection.

last_week_digest.title: No honor among thieves as 0APT threatens rival ransomware gang Krybit
last_week_digest.tldr: Ransomware gang 0APT has launched an extortion campaign against rival group Krybit, threatening to expose the identities of its affiliates. By leaking plaintext credentials and five cryptocurrency wallets, 0APT demonstrates a shift toward criminal-on-criminal warfare. This escalation signals a volatile cyber-threat landscape where internal instability among threat actors creates unpredictable risks, potentially exposing sensitive data and operational details as criminal enterprises increasingly target each other for leverage.

last_week_digest.title: CISA flags Windows Task Host vulnerability as exploited in attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: CISA has added a critical Windows Task Host privilege escalation vulnerability, CVE-2025-60710, to its actively exploited catalog, mandating that federal agencies patch their systems within two weeks. This flaw allows local attackers to gain full SYSTEM control over Windows 11 and Server 2025 devices. The development signals a broader industry trend where core system components are increasingly targeted, forcing organisations to accelerate patching cycles to mitigate significant operational risks.

last_week_digest.title: 6-Year Ransomware Campaign Targets Turkish Homes & SMBs
last_week_digest.tldr: A six-year ransomware campaign has systematically targeted Turkish households and small-to-medium businesses, exploiting the relative anonymity of smaller-scale attacks to evade detection. By avoiding high-profile enterprise targets, the threat actors maintained persistent operations, causing significant operational disruption and financial losses for numerous victims. This enduring campaign signals a concerning industry shift, highlighting how attackers increasingly favour low-profile, long-term exploitation strategies to bypass traditional security monitoring and oversight.

last_week_digest.title: Microsoft pays $2.3M for cloud and AI flaws at Zero Day Quest
last_week_digest.tldr: Microsoft awarded $2.3 million to global researchers after identifying over 80 high-impact cloud and AI vulnerabilities during its Zero Day Quest event. This initiative highlights the critical necessity of proactive bug bounty programmes in securing complex infrastructure. By addressing potential cross-tenant access and credential exposure, the company aims to rectify historical security culture concerns, signalling a broader industry shift toward prioritising rigorous, collaborative testing for emerging technologies.

last_week_digest.title: Educational company McGraw Hill says Salesforce misconfiguration led to data leak
last_week_digest.tldr: McGraw Hill recently suffered a data breach linked to a widespread Salesforce misconfiguration, exposing non-sensitive information. While the company maintains its core systems remain secure, the incident follows claims by the ShinyHunters group regarding 45 million stolen records globally. This event highlights the critical operational risks posed by third-party platform configurations, signalling that even major enterprises must rigorously audit external vendor environments to prevent significant reputational damage.

last_week_digest.title: ‘By Design’ Flaw in MCP Could Enable Widespread AI Supply Chain Attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Researchers have identified a critical design flaw in Anthropic’s Model Context Protocol that permits unsanitized commands to execute silently, potentially enabling widespread supply chain compromises across global AI environments. This vulnerability threatens the integrity of automated workflows, risking unauthorised data access and system manipulation. The discovery signals an urgent need for robust security frameworks as organisations increasingly integrate AI agents, highlighting systemic risks inherent in rapidly expanding interconnected infrastructures.

last_week_digest.title: Cookeville Regional Medical Center hospital data breach impacts 337,917 people
last_week_digest.tldr: The Rhysida ransomware group recently compromised Cookeville Regional Medical Center, exfiltrating 500GB of sensitive data and impacting 337,917 individuals. This significant breach exposed personal, financial, and medical records, forcing the institution into a complex recovery process. Such incidents underscore the escalating vulnerability of healthcare infrastructure to extortion-based attacks, signalling that critical service providers remain primary targets for cybercriminals seeking to leverage stolen data for financial gain.

last_week_digest.title: AI platform n8n abused for stealthy phishing and malware delivery
last_week_digest.tldr: Threat actors are exploiting the n8n workflow automation platform to launch sophisticated phishing campaigns and deliver malware by leveraging trusted webhooks. By masquerading as legitimate services like OneDrive, attackers bypass traditional security controls to deploy remote management tools for data exfiltration. This trend signals a critical industry shift where productivity platforms are increasingly weaponised, forcing security teams to treat flexible, low-code automation tools as significant operational liabilities.

last_week_digest.title: Recently leaked Windows zero-days now exploited in attacks
last_week_digest.tldr: Threat actors are actively exploiting three Windows zero-day vulnerabilities, known as BlueHammer, RedSun, and UnDefend, following public disclosure by a researcher. These flaws allow attackers to gain SYSTEM-level privileges and disable security updates, posing severe risks to Windows 10, 11, and Server systems. This incident highlights the growing danger of uncoordinated vulnerability disclosures, forcing businesses to manage heightened operational risks while awaiting official patches from Microsoft developers.

last_week_digest.title: Ukrainian emergency services and hospitals hit by espionage campaign using new AgingFly malware
last_week_digest.tldr: The threat group UAC-0247 has targeted Ukrainian hospitals and emergency services using the new AgingFly malware to exfiltrate sensitive data and illicitly mine cryptocurrency. By leveraging sophisticated phishing tactics and AI-generated content, attackers gained remote control over critical infrastructure systems. This campaign signals a growing trend of adversaries weaponising humanitarian themes to compromise essential public services, highlighting the urgent need for heightened vigilance across global healthcare sectors.

last_week_digest.title: Microsoft Paid Out $2.3 Million at Zero Day Quest 2026 Hacking Contest
last_week_digest.tldr: Microsoft distributed $2.3 million in rewards during its Zero Day Quest 2026 hacking contest, where security researchers identified over 80 high-impact vulnerabilities within its cloud and AI infrastructure. This substantial payout highlights the critical operational risks inherent in rapidly scaling emerging technologies. For the broader industry, the event signals a strategic shift towards proactive, incentivised bug discovery to mitigate systemic threats before they cause catastrophic global business disruptions.

last_week_digest.title: Vercel confirms breach as hackers claim to be selling stolen data
last_week_digest.tldr: Cloud development platform Vercel recently confirmed a security breach resulting from a compromised third-party AI tool’s Google Workspace OAuth application. Attackers exploited an employee account to access non-sensitive environment variables, potentially exposing customer data. This incident highlights the critical risks posed by third-party integrations and supply chain vulnerabilities, signalling that even robust platforms face significant operational threats when interconnected services lack stringent, uniform encryption for all stored variables.

last_week_digest.title: Ukraine confirms suspected APT28 campaign targeting prosecutors, anti-corruption agencies
last_week_digest.tldr: Russian-linked group APT28 has compromised over 170 email accounts belonging to Ukrainian prosecutors and anti-corruption agencies since 2023. Exploiting Roundcube vulnerabilities, the campaign targeted officials across Ukraine and neighbouring NATO states to facilitate espionage and disinformation. While internal systems remained secure, the breach highlights how state-sponsored actors leverage zero-click exploits to undermine institutional integrity, signalling a persistent, evolving threat to government communications and regional stability globally.

last_week_digest.title: Kyrgyzstan-based crypto exchange Grinex shuts down after $13.7M cyber heist, blames Western Intelligence
last_week_digest.tldr: Kyrgyzstan-based crypto exchange Grinex has permanently ceased operations following a sophisticated cyber heist that resulted in the theft of $13.7 million from Russian user wallets. The incident, which saw funds rapidly converted to evade asset freezing, highlights the increasing intersection of geopolitical conflict and digital finance. This collapse underscores the profound operational risks facing platforms operating within sanctioned jurisdictions, signalling a volatile environment for cross-border cryptocurrency services.

last_week_digest.title: Hidden VMs: how hackers leverage QEMU to stealthily steal data and spread malware
last_week_digest.tldr: Threat actors are increasingly abusing QEMU emulators to hide malware within virtual machines, effectively bypassing endpoint security controls. Sophos identified two major campaigns, STAC4713 and STAC3725, where attackers maintained persistent, covert access to exfiltrate data and deploy PayoutsKing ransomware. This trend signals a shift toward stealthier, hypervisor-based exploitation, forcing organisations to rethink traditional detection strategies as adversaries move beyond simple malware to sophisticated, long-term persistence within compromised network environments.

last_week_digest.title: Next.js Creator Vercel Hacked
last_week_digest.tldr: Vercel, the developer behind the Next.js framework, recently confirmed a security breach after hackers linked to the ShinyHunters collective attempted to sell stolen data for $2 million. This incident threatens the integrity of critical web infrastructure and highlights the growing risk of supply chain attacks targeting core development platforms. It signals an urgent industry need for heightened authentication protocols to protect sensitive source code and proprietary corporate assets.

last_week_digest.title: Adaptavist Group breach spawns imposter emails as ransomware crew claims mega-haul
last_week_digest.tldr: UK enterprise consultancy The Adaptavist Group is investigating a security breach after attackers utilised stolen credentials to infiltrate internal systems. While the firm maintains that only basic contact data was exposed, the ransomware group "The Gentlemen" claims to have stolen hundreds of thousands of records and source code. This incident highlights the growing threat of credential-based attacks and the subsequent weaponisation of stolen data through targeted phishing campaigns.

last_week_digest.title: Vercel’s security breach started with malware disguised as Roblox cheats
last_week_digest.tldr: Vercel recently suffered a security breach after an attacker compromised a Context.ai employee’s device with Lumma Stealer malware, subsequently hijacking a Vercel staff member’s Google Workspace account via OAuth tokens. This incident exposed sensitive environment variables and credentials for a limited customer base. It highlights the systemic risks of interconnected SaaS ecosystems and overly privileged third-party integrations, signalling an urgent industry need for stricter identity governance and access controls.

last_week_digest.title: Third-party AI hack triggers Vercel breach, internal environments accessed
last_week_digest.tldr: Vercel recently suffered a security breach after attackers compromised a third-party AI tool, Context.ai, to hijack an employee’s Google Workspace account. This intrusion granted unauthorised access to internal systems and non-sensitive environment variables, forcing the company to engage Mandiant for investigation. This incident highlights the growing systemic risk posed by third-party AI integrations, signalling that organisations must urgently tighten supply chain security and strictly manage third-party software permissions.

last_week_digest.title: Why the Axios attack proves AI is mandatory for supply chain security
last_week_digest.tldr: A suspected North Korean threat actor recently compromised the Axios JavaScript library, exposing approximately 100 million weekly downloads to malicious code. Although removed within three hours, the package was downloaded over half a million times. This incident confirms that software supply chain attacks are accelerating, signalling that human-speed defences are now insufficient. Consequently, adopting AI-powered security tools is no longer optional but a mandatory requirement for global operational resilience.

last_week_digest.title: Hackers Abuse QEMU for Defense Evasion
last_week_digest.tldr: Threat actors are increasingly weaponising the QEMU machine emulator to facilitate defense evasion across at least two distinct global campaigns distributing ransomware and remote access tools. This exploitation allows attackers to bypass traditional security controls, posing significant operational risks to enterprise infrastructure. Such tactics signal a concerning industry-wide trend where legitimate virtualisation software is repurposed to mask malicious activities, complicating incident response and threatening critical business continuity worldwide.

last_week_digest.title: French govt agency confirms breach as hacker offers to sell data
last_week_digest.tldr: France Titres, the agency managing national identity documents, confirmed a breach involving the ants.gouv.fr portal. While the agency investigates, a threat actor claims to have stolen 19 million records, including names and contact details. This incident highlights the persistent vulnerability of state infrastructure to identity-focused cyberattacks. It signals a growing industry trend where government databases become prime targets for mass data extortion, necessitating heightened vigilance against sophisticated phishing.

last_week_digest.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
last_week_digest.tldr: British national Tyler Buchanan has pleaded guilty to wire fraud and identity theft as a senior member of the Scattered Spider cybercrime group. His activities, involving tens of thousands of phishing attacks, compromised at least twelve major technology firms and siphoned over $8 million in cryptocurrency. This conviction highlights the escalating operational threat posed by social engineering syndicates, signalling a shift toward more robust international law enforcement cooperation.

last_week_digest.title: Ransomware negotiator caught secretly assisting BlackCat extortion scheme
last_week_digest.tldr: A former ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to conspiring with the BlackCat cybercriminal group, having secretly leaked sensitive client data across five cases to inflate extortion demands. This insider betrayal, which involved extorting $1.2 million from a single victim, highlights a critical vulnerability in incident response trust. The case signals an urgent industry need for stricter vetting and oversight of cybersecurity professionals who hold privileged access to victim data.

last_week_digest.title: $290 Million Kelp DAO Crypto Heist Blamed on North Korea
last_week_digest.tldr: North Korean state-sponsored actors successfully executed a sophisticated $290 million heist targeting Kelp DAO by compromising LayerZero’s distributed validator network infrastructure. This breach, which involved manipulating remote procedure calls to force failovers into poisoned systems, highlights the severe operational risks inherent in decentralised finance protocols. The incident signals a growing industry trend where attackers exploit complex cross-chain dependencies, forcing organisations to prioritise infrastructure resilience against persistent nation-state threats.

last_week_digest.title: Yet another ex-ransomware negotiator admits turning rogue after payoff from crimelords
last_week_digest.tldr: A former ransomware negotiator has pleaded guilty to colluding with the ALPHV/BlackCat gang, betraying five client firms by leaking confidential insurance data to inflate ransom demands. These victims, including a nonprofit that paid $26.8 million, suffered significant financial losses due to this insider threat. This case signals a critical erosion of trust in incident response services, forcing global organisations to implement stricter vetting and oversight of third-party security partners.
